stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The mention of 'safetensors' indicates the use of modern, efficient model serialization formats.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: Musk says US military suicide drones used Starlink in violation of SpaceX rules
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Musk alleges US military used Starlink for suicide drones, violating SpaceX's operational rules.
The drones reportedly used Starlink instead of the dedicated, presumably more secure, Starshield service.
Musk has placed blame for the alleged violation on a military contractor.

stories.title: Millions of AI agents imperiled by critical vulnerability in open source package
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability, dubbed "BadHost," was discovered in the Starlette package.
Starlette is a widely used open-source package, boasting 325 million weekly downloads.
The vulnerability poses a significant threat to millions of AI agents relying on this dependency.

stories.title: From Model Scaling to System Scaling: Scaling the Harness in Agentic AI
stories.author: Shangding Gu
bullet_points: The paper identifies system scaling, rather than just model scaling, as the next critical bottleneck in Agentic AI.
It proposes the concept of 'scaling the harness,' focusing on designing robust, auditable, and modular architectures around foundation models.
This shift treats the structured execution layer surrounding LLMs (the 'harness') as a primary object for design and optimization.

stories.title: DiscoverPhysics: Benchmarking LLMs for Out-of-the-Box Scientific Thinking
stories.author: Carolina Cuesta-Lázaro
bullet_points: Introduces DiscoverPhysics, a new benchmark designed to test genuine scientific reasoning in LLMs.
The benchmark challenges LLMs by having them discover the laws of motion in simulated worlds with non-standard physics (e.g., fractional-power gravity).
Addresses the critical limitation of current LLMs: distinguishing between true reasoning and mere recall of existing scientific knowledge.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It is associated with key AI technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for performance on Apple Silicon.
This integration leverages MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, for optimized execution.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open usage.

stories.title: NASA’s permanent Moon base plans start with three missions this year
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: NASA is advancing its plans for a permanent Moon base.
The initial phase involves three specific Moon Base missions this year.
These missions are foundational to the crewed Artemis landing scheduled for 2028.

stories.title: Forgetting in Language Models: Capacity, Optimization, and Self-Generated Replay
stories.author: Andrew Gordon Wilson
bullet_points: The paper addresses catastrophic forgetting in language models, where performance on old tasks degrades when learning new ones.
It proposes that language models can effectively use self-generated samples from their own training distribution as replay data to mitigate forgetting.
The research indicates that forgetting persists primarily when the model has limited remaining capacity.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports experimental local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machine.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within Ollama.
It facilitates the use of proprietary models like Claude alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Google Health is here, but a lot of people want their Fitbit app back instead
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google has officially replaced the Fitbit app with Google Health.
The transition has caused significant user confusion and frustration among Fitbit users.
The change coincides with the launch of the new Fitbit Air device.

stories.title: American Airlines is getting Starlink Wi-Fi
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: American Airlines is adopting SpaceX's Starlink Wi-Fi for in-flight connectivity.
The rollout is scheduled to begin in Q1 2027 and cover over 500 aircraft.
Starlink will be integrated alongside existing providers like Viastat and SES.

stories.title: NASA takes steps toward building Moon Base, including discussing a "perimeter"
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA is actively planning for the construction of a Moon Base.
Discussions around the base include considerations for a 'perimeter' security or operational zone.
The project development is being conducted with careful adherence to the Outer Space Treaty.

stories.title: FBI agent explains how easy it is to ID people posting AI porn without consent
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The FBI is actively investigating the identification of individuals creating and posting non-consensual AI pornography.
The investigation highlighted how easily personal information can be linked to AI porn accounts via social media platforms like Instagram.
This signals a growing focus on digital forensics and the legal challenges posed by synthetic media abuse.

stories.title: 3D-printable humanoid legs let robotics experiments run wild
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Hugging Face has launched a new $2,500 bipedal robot project.
The robot is designed to be used by builders and researchers for experimentation.
The project focuses on 3D-printable humanoid legs, enabling advanced robotics testing.

stories.title: Squeezing Capacity from Multimodal Large Language Models for Subject-driven Generation
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of subject-driven image generation, aiming to synthesize new images while preserving a specific subject's identity.
Existing methods often struggle with cross-modal reasoning and produce artifacts because they encode text and reference images separately.
The proposed work conditions diffusion models to better integrate identity preservation with textual instructions, improving upon current multimodal frameworks.

stories.title: Prism: A Plug-in Reproducible Infrastructure for Scalable Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
stories.author: Da-Wei Zhou
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Prism,' a plug-in infrastructure designed to solve engineering bottlenecks in Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT).
MCIT is a critical area of research needed for real-world deployment, allowing Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to continuously adapt to new tasks.
Current MCIT methods suffer because they require direct, complex modifications to the base MLLM codebase, which Prism aims to circumvent.

stories.title: Looped Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Dongmin Park
bullet_points: Introduces LoopMDM, a novel approach to Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs) for language modeling.
The core innovation involves selectively looping early-middle transformer layers during training.
This looping technique reportedly enhances both the training efficiency and overall performance of MDMs.

stories.title: OrpQuant: Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for Multiplier-Free Power-of-Two Transformer Quantization
stories.author: Tao Luo
bullet_points: Addresses deployment constraints of LLMs and ViTs on edge devices due to memory and MAC bottlenecks.
Proposes 'OrpQuant,' a method using Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for quantization.
Focuses on leveraging Power-of-Two (PoT) quantization to replace costly MAC operations with efficient bit-shifts.

stories.title: Cómo Evaluar Agentes IA: Tutorial de LLM-as-Judge
stories.author: elizabethfuentes12
bullet_points: Introduces the 'LLM-as-Judge' methodology for evaluating AI agents.
Focuses on detecting critical issues like silent failures, wasted tokens, and hallucinations pre-production.
Provides a practical tutorial using Python code for implementation.
